report_name: Nearly 600,000 Patients Affected by Data Breaches at Three U.S. Healthcare Providers

threat_actors.name: Insomnia
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that claimed responsibility for the breach at Southern Illinois Dermatology, employing a double-extortion strategy by leaking stolen data on its leak site.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data theft.

threat_actors.name: LockBit
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that had previously listed Saint Anthony Hospital as a victim in January 2024, indicating a history of targeting the organization. This was mentioned as historical context and unrelated to the current email compromise incident.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Considered a likely initial access vector for the network intrusions at North Texas Behavioral Health Authority and Southern Illinois Dermatology, and for the credential theft that led to the email compromise at Saint Anthony Hospital.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used by attackers to gain unauthorized access to two employee email inboxes at Saint Anthony Hospital in February 2025, after the account credentials were stolen.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: Attackers accessed files on network servers containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and Protected Health Information (PHI) at North Texas Behavioral Health Authority and Southern Illinois Dermatology.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The Insomnia ransomware group exfiltrated stolen patient data from Southern Illinois Dermatology to their own servers as part of a double-extortion scheme before leaking it.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Insomnia ransomware group encrypted files on the network of Southern Illinois Dermatology to disrupt operations and extort a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: A common precursor technique for ransomware, such as deleting volume shadow copies with vssadmin.exe, was likely used by the Insomnia group to hinder recovery efforts at Southern Illinois Dermatology.

attack_patterns.name: Email Forwarding Rule
attack_patterns.description: Attackers who compromised email accounts at Saint Anthony Hospital may have created malicious inbox rules to covertly monitor communications and exfiltrate data.

malware.name: Insomnia
malware.description: The ransomware used by the Insomnia group to conduct a double-extortion attack against Southern Illinois Dermatology, involving both data encryption and data theft.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The vssadmin.exe utility was mentioned as a tool likely used by ransomware operators like Insomnia to delete volume shadow copies and inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: North Texas Behavioral Health Authority
identities.description: A Texas-based healthcare provider that suffered a network server breach affecting 285,000 individuals between October 13 and October 15, 2025.

identities.name: Southern Illinois Dermatology
identities.description: An Illinois-based dermatology clinic that suffered a network compromise affecting 160,000 individuals. The breach was claimed by the Insomnia ransomware group in February 2026.

identities.name: Saint Anthony Hospital
identities.description: A Chicago-based hospital that experienced a data breach affecting 146,000 individuals due to a compromised email account in February 2025.

identities.name: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
identities.description: The U.S. federal agency to which the three healthcare organizations reported their data breaches.

infrastructure.name: Insomnia Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Insomnia ransomware group to publish and leak data stolen from victims, such as Southern Illinois Dermatology.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1c9d-4e5f-4a1b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware deployment.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/data-breaches-at-healthcare-organizations-in-illinois-and-texas-affect-600000/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used to delete volume shadow copies, a technique often used by ransomware like Insomnia to prevent easy system recovery.

indicators.name: Malicious Email Forwarding Rule Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Malicious Email Forwarding Rule Creation
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-3d2c-1b0a-987654321fed
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of new inbox rules in Microsoft 365/Exchange, which can be used by attackers to exfiltrate data from compromised mailboxes.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/data-breaches-at-healthcare-organizations-in-illinois-and-texas-affect-600000/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1114.003
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.exchange
detection:
  selection:
    Operation|contains:
      - 'New-InboxRule'
      - 'Set-InboxRule'
    Parameters|contains: 'ForwardTo'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of email forwarding rules, a potential action taken by attackers after compromising email accounts, such as in the Saint Anthony Hospital incident.
